title: The 28 Years Later Team Discusses Their Past Creative Differences
text: Danny Boyle and Alex Garland have a well-documented creative partnership history spanning back to their work on 28 Days Later. During that earlier collaboration, Garland favored ambiguity in storytelling, while Boyle preferred definitive explanations. The tension came to a head when Boyle wrote a prologue attempting to explain everything. Garland responded by saying, If you f***ing put that on the film, I'm off. Garland even wrote a note to Boyle saying, I'm sorry we had such a miserable time together, and I hope you have a much better time on the next film, a note Boyle still keeps. Despite this rocky history, the duo managed to reconcile and collaborate again on the 28 Years Later trilogy. Garland explained that the story was narratively ambitious and they tried to condense it, but its natural form felt like a trilogy. For The Bone Temple, Garland decided to write the script and hand it over, stepping back to give Nia DaCosta total creative space as director. This approach reflected Garland's belief that as a director, he wouldn't want someone on set dictating creative choices.
